name: Potstickers
recipeIngredient: 40 each Won-ton wrappers, Cut into circles
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine ground pork, cabbage, onions, egg, soy sauce, salt, orange peel, and hot chili oil in a large bowl and mix well.
2. Dust waxed or parchment paper with cornstarch.
3. Place 1 rounded teaspoon of filling in the center of each wonton wrapper, pressing lightly so the filling forms a narrow band across the middle.
4. Moisten the rim of the skin.
5. Bring opposite sides together to form a semicircle and pinch around the outer edge.
6. Transfer to cornstarch-dusted paper.
7. Repeat with remaining dumplings and filling.
8. Place two heavy 12-inch skillets over low heat. Add 1/4 cup oil to each.
9. Arrange dumplings in rows, fitting closely together, in the skillets.
10. Increase heat to medium-high and cook uncovered until bottoms are deeply golden, about 2 minutes, checking occasionally.
11. Add 1/2 cup water to each pan and cover immediately. Let steam until skins are translucent, about 3 minutes.
12. Remove cover and continue cooking over medium to medium-high heat until bottoms are very crisp and well browned.
13. Drain off excess oil if necessary.
14. Loosen dumplings with a spatula and transfer to a serving dish. Serve immediately.
